candidate_problems.text: Can 'low freedom of informational substitution without loss of explanatory power' be operationalized into a test that separates genuine explanations from merely coherent narratives across multiple domains?
How should counterfactual fragility be balanced against robustness, given that some good explanations tolerate variation in presentation while preserving the same underlying structure?
Do dense internal constraints reliably produce broad external reach, or can there be tightly constrained explanations that remain narrow and non-generative?
What are the right units of analysis for informational structure—objects, relations, symmetries, invariants, criticisms—and can different choices change whether an explanation appears hard to vary?
